message:
[LOW] fcp-sandbox: fix test_noop_sandbox_verify_* state_dir wiring

test_noop_sandbox_verify_file_access (sandbox.rs:942) and
test_noop_sandbox_verify_all_operations (sandbox.rs:1617) both called
`CompiledPolicy::from_manifest(&section, None)` and then asserted that
writing to "/var/lib/fcp/connectors/test/cache.db" was allowed.

That cannot hold: the fixture sets `fs_writable_paths: ["$CONNECTOR_STATE"]`,
and `expand_path` (sandbox.rs:254) resolves `$CONNECTOR_STATE` against
the `state_dir` argument — passing `None` collapses the placeholder
to `None` and drops the entry entirely. With an empty writable set,
verify_file_access_policy correctly refuses the write, so both
assertions fired.

The intent was clearly "NoOp sandbox still consults the populated
policy", so pass `Some(PathBuf::from("/var/lib/fcp/connectors/test"))`
matching the state_dir already used in test_compile_policy a few
functions up. No behavior change; purely a fixture correction.

Verified: full fcp-sandbox --lib now 557/557 green (was 555/557 with
these two spuriously failing). Surfaced during Cycle 2 of the p6
review but not committed then because a concurrent fcp-crypto WIP
was blocking cargo test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
